title: Platanthera dyeriana

intro: Platanthera dyeriana, commonly known as Dyer's orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Platanthera dyeriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often made into tea or tincture, and is also used in herbal soups and infusions to promote respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
